## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. And today we're going to talk about the potential reasons for a drop in ad costs. Let's go. For the fifth straight quarter, MetaAd prices dropped year over year. How is that possible? I think there are five potential explanations. First, user growth. Growth for the first quarter was only about 5%, but that still contributes to additional inventory. Second, user activity. Amazingly, daily active users versus monthly active users hit a record 68% in the first quarter. But this increased activity leads to more inventory. Third, more ads shown. The most popular and controversial theory is that Meta is just trying to squeeze more ads within the news feed. It's possible, but it's difficult to prove. Fourth, more placements. Meta has added a few different placements during the past year. That increases the inventory. And fifth, Advantage Plus placements. Advertisers are more commonly using secondary placements now. By being more willing to use those secondary placements, the average cost per ad will go down. Some, or possibly all five of these, contribute to a drop in ad costs. What do you think? Last call. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
